title: NBTC to embed OTT oversight in plan
desc: The National Broadcasting and Telecommunications Commission (NBTC) has reached a consensus that it has the authority to regulate over-the-top (OTT) platforms, and will delete this oversight in its upcoming Broadcasting and Television Master Plan (2026–2030).

title: Expert doubts viability of  govt policies
desc: A political science scholar is questioning the viability of the new government's policy platform, despite acknowledging its broad scope in addressing national challenges.

title: Julapun pledges aid for slain crew's kin
desc: Labour Minister Julapun Amornvivat expressed condolences to the families of three Thai crew members of the MV Mayuree Naree who were confirmed killed in an attack on the vessel in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: NACC delivers evidence  in MPs' lese-majeste case
desc: The National Anti-Corruption Commission (NACC) delivered case files to the Supreme Court in a high-profile ethics case against 44 former Move Forward Party (MFP) MPs who signed a proposal to amend Section 112 of the Criminal Code, the lese majeste law.

title: Commission okays drone regulation reforms
desc: The National Broadcasting and Telecommunications Commission (NBTC) has approved a new set of draft regulations to expand drone spectrum use, upgrade technical standards, and strengthen oversight of drone operations nationwide.

title: Commission ramps up crackdown on investment scams
desc: The Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has intensified law enforcement actions to reinforce market credibility, as a sharp rise in investment scams, largely driven by social media, emerges as a growing systemic risk.

title: Premier ducks queries on legal action by refiners
desc: Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul declined to directly address concerns over private refiners preparing legal action against the government after it ordered a reduction in diesel refining margins, instead referring reporters to Energy Minister Akanat Promphan for clarification.

title: MPs grill govt on fuel plan
desc: The energy crisis and soaring fuel prices -- particularly the issue of refining margins -- dominated parliamentary debate during a joint sitting in parliament on Thursday, as opposition parties challenged the government's policy statement.

title: Pratunam flyover to close for 10 months for Orange Line construction
desc: The Pratunam flyover on Phetchaburi Road, Bangkok, will be closed to traffic for about 10 months from late April as tunnelling for the Orange Line enters a critical underground phase, the Mass Rapid Transit Authority of Thailand (MRTA) announced on Thursday.

title: Surging costs dull Songkran spirits
desc: Soaring fuel costs are rippling across Thailand just days before the Songkran festival, pushing up food prices, squeezing small vendors, raising costs for farmers and transport operators, and dampening travel sentiment in key tourism centres.

title: Merchant in hot water over 'fake gems'
desc: The Consumer Protection Police Division (CPPD) has arrested “Nicha”, a well-known online vendor of luxury goods, for allegedly deceiving customers by selling synthetic diamonds marketed as natural, in a case with reportedly more than 100 victims.

title: Fake coconut water alert after factories inspected
desc: The Ombudsman has called for an accelerated crackdown on the production of fake coconut water, following the arrest of operators at two processing facilities in Samut Songkhram province.

title: Thailand seizes B8.27bn more from regional scam network
desc: Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul on Thursday announced the seizure and freezing of a further 8.27 billion baht in assets linked to a transnational scam network, pushing the total value of confiscated assets in the case to more than 20 billion baht.

title: Songkran spending set to fall this year
desc: The University of the Thai Chamber of Commerce (UTCC) has revised down its forecast for this year’s Songkran spending to as low as 120 billion baht, from a previous 129.6 billion baht, citing rising diesel prices.

title: Tourism minister chasing B3-trillion goal
desc: Thailand’s tourism minister has vowed to restore tourism revenue to more than 3 trillion baht, exceeding pre-pandemic levels within the new government’s four-year term, and will proceed with a 300-baht tourism fee, as adopted in many countries.

title: Songkran 2026 around Bangkok: where to make merit then get soaked
desc: Songkran in 2026 is best tackled like Bangkok traffic: pick your lane early, commit, and keep a dry change of clothes within reach. The Thai New Year’s water fights still dominate the headlines, but this year’s smartest day trips mix temple-time calm with big-city spectacle, from Rattanakosin’s most photogenic blessings to Samut Prakan’s late-April Mon traditions —...

title: Policy statement lacks heart and clarity, says Abhisit
desc: Democrat Party leader Abhisit Vejjajiva on Thursday delivered a scathing critique of Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul’s policy statement, accusing the government of lacking clarity, substance and empathy for the public, while failing to address pressing security and economic challenges.

title: Central bank chief expects no change in interest rate
desc: The Bank of Thailand will keep its policy interest rate ​on hold, with ‌economic growth projected at 1.3% to 1.7% this year, governor Vitai Ratanakorn said on Thursday.

title: New movies out this week: Apr 9-15
desc: Five new releases that hit cinemas in Thailand this week.

title: Miss Grand Thailand contestant secures global attention with hip hop dance
desc: Miss Grand Thailand contestant secures global attention with hip hop dance Legacy A contestant from Kalasin province gained international attention after a viral hip hop dance moves during the Miss Grand Thailand 2026 competition in Bangkok on March 28.

title: Two killed as Udon Thani wildfire crew pickup hits power pole
desc: Two killed as Udon Thani wildfire crew pickup hits power pole Legacy A pickup truck carrying park officials back from a wildfire operation crashed into a power pole on a sharp bend in Udon Thani yesterday evening, April 8, leaving two men dead and four others injured.

title: Thai stepfather kills toddler, tries to revive him with electric shock
desc: Thai stepfather kills toddler, tries to revive him with electric shock Legacy A Thai stepfather confessed to fatally abusing a one year old boy and shocking the child with an electric fishing device in an attempt to revive him in Ratchaburi.

title: Fake diamond seller arrested over 4 million baht losses
desc: The owner of a jewellery sales page with nearly 100,000 followers was arrested today, April 9, for allegedly selling fake diamonds and gems through livestreams... Pathum Thani.

title: Gold prices projected to hit $6,000 by third quarter
desc: Gold prices are expected to reach US$6,000 an ounce in the third quarter of this year... even though short-term volatility is expected amid lingering uncertainties, traders say.

title: Butler breaks silence on Teresa Teng’s death in Chiang Mai 31 years on
desc: A Thai man, who said he was the butler of Taiwanese singer Teng Li-Chun , recalled the events before her death in Chiang Mai...

title: 中国光模块巨头Terahop扩展在泰规模 本地采购超50%
desc: 泰国投资促进委员会（BOI）秘书长纳立表示，他参加并庆祝了Terahop（泰国）有限公司在泰运营5周年活动... 该公司为中国企业...

title: Mercedes driver doges petrol bill, station worker forced to cover costs
desc: Mercedes driver doges petrol bill, station worker forced to cover costs Legacy A woman driving a white Mercedes-Benz allegedly left a petrol station in Thung Yai, Hat Yai, Songkhla, with a 1,000 baht fuel bill unpaid on April 6, leaving the employee to cover the cost himself . The incident occurred at 2.42pm when a woman aged around 30 to 35 arrived in a white Mercedes-Benz and asked to fill benzine 95 worth 2,000 baht. She reportedly later reduced the amount to 1,000 baht as the pump was dispensing slowly. Photo via Khaosod An employee, 23 year old Theerapong, said the woman attempted to pay

title: Prospect sees e-commerce driving warehouse demand
desc: Industrial estate developer Prospect Development expects greater diversification among global suppliers relocating to Thailand, alongside continued growth in e-commerce, to drive demand for ready-built factories and warehouse space.

title: Kao launches Attack campaign showcasing ‘Fragrance Capsule Technology’ for long-lasting freshness
desc: Kao launches Attack campaign showcasing ‘Fragrance Capsule Technology’ for long-lasting freshness Legacy Kao Industrial (Thailand) Co., Ltd. , a Japanese manufacturer of consumer and chemical products, has launched a new campaign for Attack laundry detergent under the concept “To make clothes more fragrant, this moment calls for Attack: Pat, Pull, and Hug.” The company said the campaign highlights Fragrance Capsule Technology , designed to release fragrance throughout the day through physical movement, targeting modern consumers, including working families and younger people with f

title: Police detain 36 Chinese nationals in Pattaya nightclub raid
desc: Police detain 36 Chinese nationals in Pattaya nightclub raid Legacy Police in Pattaya raided a nightclub in Nong Prue, Bang Lamung, Chon Buri early today, April 9, arresting 36 Chinese nationals suspected of gathering for drug use . The operation took place at 3.30am after police received a tip-off about alleged drug activity inside a venue on Pattaya Third Road in North Pattaya.

title: Israeli national arrested for nominee-run travel agency in Phuket
desc: Israeli national arrested for nominee-run travel agency in Phuket Legacy Police arrested an Israeli national in Phuket yesterday, April 8, for operating a travel agency illegally through a Thai nominee . Officers from the Department of Tourism and Tourist Police investigated illegal travel agencies in Phuket and found two companies in breach of the law.

title: 宋干节期间泰国各大机场推出停车福利迎出行高峰
desc: 泰国头条新闻社 据泰媒报道，4月8日，泰国机场集团（AOT）CEO帕维娜表示，宋干节期间民众返乡与旅游出行增加。

title: 泰国新政府向国会提交施政纲要
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月9日，泰国总理兼内政部长阿努廷在国会大厦发表政府施政纲要，阐述内阁未来施政框架。

title: Mai Davika以新角色亮相《冰湖重生》发布会
desc: 泰国头条新闻社 据泰媒报道，4月8日，泰国人气演员Mai Davika飞赴海外，出席中国大型剧集《冰湖重生》发布会。

title: 芭提雅警方突袭KTV会所 36名中国人疑涉吸毒被捕
desc: 泰国头条新闻社 据泰媒报道，4月9日凌晨，芭堤雅市警察局局长率队，会同泰国东部二区侦查人员，动用40余名警力，对芭堤雅北部Pattaya Sai 3路一处俱乐部实施了包围搜查。

title: 王毅会见泰国公主诗琳通
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 2026年4月8日，中共中央政治局委员、外交部长王毅在北京会见泰国公主诗琳通。
